---
title: "Math 3190 Homework"
subtitle: "Fundamentals of Data Science &middot; Southern Utah University"
author: "Melanie Jensen"
---

Welcome! This website is where I share the coursework I've completed for **Math 3190: Fundamentals of Data Science** at Southern Utah University, taught by Professor Rick Brown. Each homework assignment is rendered from a Quarto (`.qmd`) document, with code, output, and written answers all in one place.

## About the course

Math 3190 is a hands-on introduction to the tools and methods of data science. Across ten assignments, the course moves through data wrangling with the `tidyverse`, querying databases with SQL, resampling methods like bootstrapping, regression and regularization, dimension reduction, classification, and time series analysis.

All of the work on this site is done in **R** with **Quarto**, and version-controlled in **Git** &mdash; which means every assignment here reflects not just the statistics, but also the workflow of reproducible, collaborative data science.

## What you'll find here

- **[Homework](Homework/index.qmd)** &mdash; all ten assignments, each with the full write-up, code, and output.
- **[About](about.qmd)** &mdash; a bit more about me, the course, and the tools I've been learning.
- **[GitHub repo](https://github.com/mjens328/Math_3190_Homework_Assignments)** &mdash; the source `.qmd` files and everything that builds this site.

Thanks for stopping by!
